title: Runabout: A mobile EEG study of auditory oddball processing in laboratory and real-world conditions
description: This mobile EEG dataset investigates attentional processing during an auditory oddball task across three environmental conditions: laboratory, outdoor field, and naturalistic campus navigation. Forty-four healthy adults performed the task under both attended (counting deviant tones) and ignored stimulus conditions, with 32-channel EEG recordings capturing neural responses to complex tone stimuli. The study examines how cognitive task demands, motor demands, and environmental complexity modulate event-related potentials and behavioral performance.
methods_description: EEG signals were recorded from 32 active electrodes using a Brain Vision LiveAmp 32 amplifier. Participants performed an oddball task with complex tone stimuli (piano and horn) in three settings: sitting in a quiet laboratory, walking around a sports field, and navigating a university campus route. Each environmental condition was performed twice: once while attending to oddball stimuli (counting deviant tones) and once while ignoring the stimuli. Data were preprocessed using EEGLAB and ERPLAB toolboxes with independent component analysis for artifact removal.
